file_name: PSGT Agreement NDA New Supplier Non Disclosure Template DC.txt

perturbation.original_text: Upon the termination of the Services for any reas on or at the written request of a Disclosing Party at any time, the Supplier shall destroy (and promptly deliver a certification of such destruction) or promptly deliver to each Disclosing Party, or to the requesting Disclosing Party as the case may be, all tangible embodiments of such Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information in the Supplier’s or its employees’ possession, including but no t limited to copies, notes, packages, pictures, diagrams, computer memory media and all other tangible embodiments of, or materials containing, any portion of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the termination of the Services for any reason or at the written request of a Disclosing Party, the Supplier may, at its discretion, either destroy (and promptly deliver a certification of such destruction) or retain copies of tangible embodiments of such Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information in the Supplier’s or its employees’ possession for archival purposes, unless otherwise instructed by CGSH in writing. In the event of retention, the Supplier shall ensure continued confidentiality according to this agreement. Instructions from CGSH to deliver confidential information will be subject to a fee.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates that the Supplier *shall* destroy or return all Confidential Information upon termination. The changed text makes this action discretionary by stating the Supplier *may* destroy or retain copies, thus contradicting the original mandatory requirement. By making the instruction from CGSH to deliver confidential information subject to a fee, is contradicting with the initial obligation the supplier has to destroy said Confidential Information, generating uncertainty on whether the supplier should pay for a duty that it must fulfill as stated in the original agreement. This creates uncertainty about the Supplier's obligations after termination and whether confidential data will continue to be protected.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon the termination of the Services for any reason or at the written request of a Disclosing Party, the Supplier may, at its discretion, either destroy (and promptly deliver a certification of such destruction) or retain copies of tangible embodiments of such Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information in the Supplier’s or its employees’ possession for archival purposes, unless otherwise instructed by CGSH in writing. In the event of retention, the Supplier shall ensure continued confidentiality according to this agreement. Instructions from CGSH to deliver confidential information will be subject to a fee.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Supplier *shall* destroy or return all Confidential Information upon termination, while the changed text states the Supplier *may* destroy or retain copies, contradicting the mandatory requirement. Furthermore, the addition of 'Instructions from CGSH to deliver confidential information will be subject to a fee' introduces uncertainty and a direct conflict with the initial obligation, as it is unclear whether the supplier should pay for fulfilling a duty that was previously stipulated without cost. This is a clear contradiction as it alters the Supplier's obligations and introduces a fee where none previously existed, thus making it a Structural Flaw - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Supplier must therefore ensure, by way of written contract between it and its employees, subcontractors and individuals visiting the Premises on the Supplier’s behalf (each a “Contractor” and, toget her, the “Contractors”), that if at any time a Contractor becomes aware of Protected Data the Su pplier or its Contractors will not (i) share this information with anyone outside of CGSH, (ii) take any information away from the Premises, and/or (iii) duplicate or copy any Protected Data.
perturbation.changed_text: The Supplier will make a reasonable effort to ensure, verbally, that its employees, subcontractors and individuals visiting the Premises on the Supplier’s behalf (each a “Contractor” and, toget her, the “Contractors”), that if at any time a Contractor becomes aware of Protected Data the Supplier or its Contractors may share this information with individuals outside of CGSH, to the extent that those individuals have a need to know. Contractors are permitted to take information away from the premises, in the event that they believe the information will be needed to achieve the goal of the project. Data can be duplicated if it does not create any risk to CGSH.
perturbation.explanation: The original section mandates the Supplier to ensure that its contractors do not share, take away, or duplicate Protected Data and that this must be done through a written contract between the Supplier and its Contractors. The modified section directly contradicts this by stating the Supplier will make a reasonable effort and only verbally and that they may share information with outside parties, take information away from the premises, and duplicate data, under certain circumstances. Thus creating an in-text contradiction. The contradiction introduces uncertainty on whether data needs to be protected and whether the supplier is liable for data breaches due to employees sharing this data.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Supplier will make a reasonable effort to ensure, verbally, that its employees, subcontractors and individuals visiting the Premises on the Supplier’s behalf (each a “Contractor” and, toget her, the “Contractors”), that if at any time a Contractor becomes aware of Protected Data the Supplier or its Contractors may share this information with individuals outside of CGSH, to the extent that those individuals have a need to know. Contractors are permitted to take information away from the premises, in the event that they believe the information will be needed to achieve the goal of the project. Data can be duplicated if it does not create any risk to CGSH.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates a written contract ensuring contractors do not share, take away, or duplicate Protected Data. The changed text contradicts this by stating the Supplier will make a reasonable effort, only verbally, and that contractors may share information, take information away, and duplicate data under certain circumstances. This creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text, as the contradicted text allows actions that the original text strictly prohibits, based on the explanation, creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement and the obligations herein shall survive indefinitely, regardless of whether or not the Supplier is still providi ng Services at the Premises.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement and the obligations herein shall survive for a period of one year from the termination of services, unless otherwise agreed upon in writing by both parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the agreement survives indefinitely. The changed text limits the survival to one year, creating a direct contradiction. This creates uncertainty about how long confidentiality obligations persist, affecting both parties' rights and responsibilities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement and the obligations herein shall survive for a period of one year from the termination of services, unless otherwise agreed upon in writing by both parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement survives indefinitely, while the changed text limits the survival to one year. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, which means the change from indefinite survival to a one-year survival period creates a direct contradiction with the initial agreement by altering the survival terms. This falls under the definition of a structural flaw because the clause is placed with conflicting implications, creating uncertainty.

perturbation.original_text: The Supplier acknowledges that the unauthori zed use or disclosure of a Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information or any breach of this Agreement by the Supplier may cause such Disclosing Party to suffer irreparable injury not compensable by monetary damages and for which such Disclosing Party may not have an adequate remedy at law. Accordingly, the Supplier agrees that CGSH will have the right to seek, wi thout the posting of any bond or security or the giving of any undertaking as to damages or otherwi se, specific performance or other injunctive or equitable relief from a court of competent juri sdiction as may be necessary or desirable to prevent, curtail or remedy any such unauthorized us e or disclosure, whether threatened or actual.
perturbation.changed_text: The Supplier acknowledges that the unauthorized use or disclosure of a Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information or any breach of this Agreement by the Supplier may cause such Disclosing Party to suffer monetary damages that are easily calculable. Accordingly, the Supplier agrees that CGSH will have the right to seek, with the posting of a bond or security and the giving of an undertaking as to damages, monetary compensation from a court of competent jurisdiction as may be necessary or desirable to prevent, curtail or remedy any such unauthorized use or disclosure, whether threatened or actual.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that unauthorized disclosure may cause irreparable injury not compensable by monetary damages, implying that other remedies, like injunctions, are appropriate and will be sought without a bond. The modified version directly contradicts this by stating that damages are easily calculable in monetary terms and that CGSH will only seek monetary compensation with a bond and undertaking as to damages. The in-text contradiction introduces uncertainty about the types of remedies available and how CGSH can seek redress.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Supplier acknowledges that the unauthorized use or disclosure of a Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information or any breach of this Agreement by the Supplier may cause such Disclosing Party to suffer monetary damages that are easily calculable. Accordingly, the Supplier agrees that CGSH will have the right to seek, with the posting of a bond or security and the giving of an undertaking as to damages, monetary compensation from a court of competent jurisdiction as may be necessary or desirable to prevent, curtail or remedy any such unauthorized use or disclosure, whether threatened or actual.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text and the contradicted text. The original text states that unauthorized disclosure may cause irreparable injury not compensable by monetary damages and that CGSH will have the right to seek specific performance or other injunctive or equitable relief. The changed text states that damages are easily calculable in monetary terms and that CGSH will only seek monetary compensation with a bond and undertaking as to damages. The contradicted text repeats the changed text verbatim. This change creates an in-text contradiction about the types of remedies available, which aligns with the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' as the clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: To the extent any applicable Services Agreement contains terms with respect to confidentiality that conflict with, or are less restrictive with re spect to the Supplier’s obligations than, those set forth in Sections 1-3 herein, the terms of this Agreement govern.
perturbation.changed_text: To the extent any applicable Services Agreement contains terms with respect to confidentiality that conflict with, or are less restrictive with respect to the Supplier’s obligations than, those set forth in Sections 1-3 herein, the terms of the Services Agreement shall govern.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that the Non-Disclosure Agreement governs in case of conflict. The changed clause states that the Services Agreement governs in case of conflict. Thus, creating an in-text contradiction. This internal contradiction causes confusion and makes it unclear which agreement takes precedence regarding confidentiality, potentially leading to disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: To the extent any applicable Services Agreement contains terms with respect to confidentiality that conflict with, or are less restrictive with respect to the Supplier’s obligations than, those set forth in Sections 1-3 herein, the terms of the Services Agreement shall govern.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Non-Disclosure Agreement governs in case of conflict regarding confidentiality. The changed text explicitly states the Services Agreement governs in such cases, which is contradictory. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, meaning the change introduced a direct conflict regarding which agreement takes precedence on confidentiality matters, which aligns with the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition.
